title: Internal-Only Comments
excerpt: We use shared boards a lot to manage client projects. In these instances, our staff are internal members and clients have Guest access to the board. There are often instances where we would like to ask questions or share…

posts.author: sharvey (Simon Harvey)
posts.cookedHtml: We use shared boards a lot to manage client projects. In these instances, our staff are internal members and clients have Guest access to the board. There are often instances where we would like to ask questions or share thoughts on a task internally without the client being able to see them. In Jira this is possible with tickets using their “Internal Comment” option. I would like to see something similar in Monday so that most comments are public, but when required a comment can be flagged as internal and is no visible to Board Guests.

posts.author: sharvey (Simon Harvey)
posts.cookedHtml: Since I posted this about a month ago I don’t seem to see any real response from anyone. Do I take it that this is not a general problem. Am I missing some work-around that others are using to hide internal comments from external contributors?

posts.author: StephTurf (Steph)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been asking for this exact type of feature too. I would like my client to see the progress on their work but not which staff member and what they’re saying (we all work out in the field, so messaging is a key feature internally to keep everyone in the loop). Instead I think they’ve offered that I put a text column in and hide that from view for clients and my staff can write in the instead.

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml: I can see a real benefit here. Now we comment in an item when the client is ‘allowed’ to see it. The rest of the conversation takes place on Slack with an url to the monday item. This makes the comment stream in monday incomplete.

posts.author: paul.whitaker (Paul Whitaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is important to my company’s expansion to how we use the service.

I recently got a customer comment like “I’m not sure I need to see your internal back and forth”.

We would actually add a few licenses to our account if this were a feature - we have some users contributing as guest which we would want to have internal feedback capability.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
The only reason we still use JIRA integrated with our monday.com is to have a completely internal linked system for just such internal conversations.

We would stop using JIRA entirely if there was an internal only discussion feature (and not only for Enterprise level). It would need to be very obvious that you are replying only internally so team members don’t accidentally expose internal discussions to Guests. So I’m imagining a separate “Internal Notes” Item view panel, rather than a setting you have to remember to click when making an Update.

posts.author: MonDude (MonDude)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a great idea. Salesforce has similar functionality, with a “public” checkbox, so that you have to make a deliberate effort to publicize a comment, to ensure that nothing internal gets out to clients.

This seems like it would be fairly easy, and would be very, very helpful. Thank you.

posts.author: johan.n (Johan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I mentioned this request in a different thread. Feature Request: Private Notes / Hide from Guests - #9 by JordanD
And yes, internal comment feature like Jira’s would be awesome!

posts.author: scottrichins (Scott Richins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Definitely need something like this. (Hated a lot about Basecamp, but really loved this feature.) Without it, I resort to creating a separate board for internal use, but then the project is split unnecessarily. Or I send emails with links to the relevant pulse.

Neither is awesome. Private comments or internal comments would be huge for my workflow.

posts.author: sharvey (Simon Harvey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Wow, it seems as though this is a really niche request, I can’t believe that after this period of time there are only 12 up-votes.

Does no-one ever see the need to hide internal comments from board guests? If not, how are you solving this problem?

If you do, then please add your vote otherwise we will never get this resolved.

posts.author: MrK (Roban Kearby)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is easily the top feature we feel is missing from the platform. We simply want the way Basecamp does it (where a thread can be flagged as internal or client-facing) to exist within this tool, as well. Seriously, just ‘copy-paste’ that idea into Monday.com.

I want to clarify, just in case, that we do understand that we can lock / hide columns. This is not that helpful. We want to lock / hide entire rows of conversational / internal interaction.

As others have noted, our process right now is to have our internal messaging occurring in slack while we use Monday.com as the more client-facing aspect of an engagement. This is not optimal for a variety of reasons.

posts.author: Video.Company (Liz Towery)
posts.cookedHtml: I run a video production company and we have considered switching to Monday.com for several years and keep checking back to see if they’ve added the internal communications feature. I can only imagine, we’re one of MANY other creative agencies that would consider switching if this feature was added. ARE YOU LISTENING MONDAY.COM?

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: Any chance this will be added to monday’s roadmap. Looks like there are two threads going on the same topic, Feature Request: Private Notes / Hide from Guests.

posts.author: DonnoC (Donno Cole)
posts.cookedHtml: This type of feature is imperative to how we are using Monday to manage project related issues…but alas, we’ve had to resort to other methods to internally collaborate on items without the guest users (customers) seeing our internal conversations, updates, etc.

posts.author: alex.scandler (alex)
posts.cookedHtml:
I dont agree with you I think that the boards of Monday are Perfect .
I apriciate you , but this is my opinion .

posts.author: nicolaspratt (Nicolas Pratt)
posts.cookedHtml: This internal-only notes option is hugely important for our company to grow our Monday.com use, would love some movement on this from Monday?

posts.author: hughmacken (Hugh M.)
posts.cookedHtml: This is an important feature for us. Please consider adding it to Monday. Thank you!

posts.author: tcmarimosa (Tom Crawley)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature will really decide whether or not we continue to use Monday. Whilst we can have internal and external boards, it’s a bit of a faff if I’m honest!

posts.author: Admeirel (Adolpho Meirelles)
posts.cookedHtml:
@CSG @CXS @Dani hey guys, this would also be really interesting for us. Do you have any updates on this feature?

Thanks!

posts.author: GRoss (Greg)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this feature would allow us to share much more with our clients. Any updates would be appreciated. Not only would it help us as existing clients, I think it would also benefit Monday to have your boards shared as widely as possible for exposure to the platform.

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey all. I found bit of a hack but it’s far from ideal but might be useful for some of you in some use cases.

If you add the “emails and activities” item view

Then add a note and tag someone. The clients cannot see that. I tried it setting up a test guest.

You cannot have threads just single comments but they are private and connected with the item.

posts.author: hughmacken (Hugh M.)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this by chance?

posts.author: wyrobot (peter)
posts.cookedHtml: We have information about how work is produced that is either irrelevant, proprietary or we just don’t want our clients to see the information. It would be nice if the board team owners could make notes hidden from guests or even more granular permissions.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,
what about if you hide those columns with specific information ?

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @wyrobot

Yes, what about using the column level permissions? You have these on the Pro plan (not sure if you’re on that plan or not)?

Support

Board Permissions

3 min read Feature   We offer several different types of permissions on monday.com—account permissions, workspace permissions, board types, board permissions, and column permissions. In this a...

Let us know if these might work for you?

posts.author: wyrobot (peter)
posts.cookedHtml: Sure, I could do that, but I was thinking that the notes boxes include rich text, links, checkboxes and makes for a better experience.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml: OK, so rather than put the information in columns themselves you’d rather have the option to hide certain items altogether from guests or the information in the updates section?

posts.author: wyrobot (peter)
posts.cookedHtml: yes, that’s right. Some tasks require instruction that needs more elaboration than is easy to do in a column.

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: Oh, yes, would love that feature. We end up having to set up 2 boards, one for internal communications and one to include guests (clients). Would really like that feature!!!

posts.author: btaylor76 (Ben P Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I love this idea. I would say an option to not allow a guest to see any update area of any item not assigned to them would make sense. This could even be tied to the existing Board Permissions so when Edit Content was selected guests could see and edit the update area of items not assigned to them. If Edit Rows Assigned to Them is set them they could only view the details of those tickets assigned to them.

posts.author: JordanD (Jordan Di Conti)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Julia -

The issue I’ve found with the permissions is that automations do not work with columns that are hidden. I have a similar issue where I want to hide certain columns but I have automations that involve the columns I want to hide. I’m being forced to choose between hiding information or having things automated when I really want the ability to do both.

posts.author: johan.n (Johan)
posts.cookedHtml:
We would also have great use for some kind of ‘internal comment’. For example, we are working on a development task and keepinga dialogue with the client in the Update section. However, it can happen that I want to post a comment/update only to the dev team that is not relevant to the customer for some reason.
Jira had this feature where you could tick a box next to the comment form saying “restrict comment to team” or something.

Keeping separate boards just for internal comments sounds like a mess @DF2019 but I see why you do it

Hiding the whole column/section to a guest doesn’t really do it either, as I want to keep all dialogue in one single thread.

posts.author: DF2019 (David Fischer)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, having 2 boards is a pain, but it does somewhat work. The best and easiest option would be to have a “hide comment from client” checkbox for each comment posted.

posts.author: hughmacken (Hugh M.)
posts.cookedHtml: Just wanted to add my +1 to permissions at board view level rather than or in addition to at column level. We manage data related to multiple clients and vendors in a single board and then provide each client or vendor with a view of only items related to them. Creating separate boards for each of them would create way too much work and would defeat the point of being able to manage everything easily.

posts.author: Vikram (Vikram Kanabarkar)
posts.cookedHtml:
@johan.n Agreed!! Even I’m having this query now. Don’t want 2 different boards but on the same item updates restricting some for guest/client users.
Have you figured any workaround? If so would be very useful if you let me know.

posts.author: tara (tara)
posts.cookedHtml: Totally agree!! There should be an update column in addition to the update bubble on the item itself. That way you can have internal comments that can be hidden from the client. We also have 2 boards - one to work of and one for each client. There are some automations that can automate what we need but not everything so I am constantly switching between boards to make sure the relevant client comments go to my working board.

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have not tested the visibility question. However, according to the documentation, the new CommuniHub monday app notes should only be visible to members.

IMO, this is still not the optimal solution. But, until something else is made available, this might work for some.

posts.author: zensystems (AJ Howard-Dobson)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be such a great feature, we’re also resorting to internal/external boards at the moment which gets really messy and confusing.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @zensystems !

You can use Monday Private Notes.

After you install it, you’d be able to add a “Private Notes” views to the board, you can control approved users on each view and manage notes.

Hope this helps.

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: @Yoggie is this app still available?

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)

image1094×733 38.4 KB

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Kristinemd13 !

Yes it’s permissions based notes for your board items. You’d be able to add it as a view and control who can see and edit notes.

I don’t know why you can’t find it after installation, one thing I’ve notices it your screenshot says “item Views Center” whereas on other Monday account’s I’ve seen it suppose to be under “Views Center”.

You can contact me at Yogev@Formrally.com if you need further assistance.

posts.author: paulronolo (Paul)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Yoggie, I tried your app and it’s great! Is it only available in board view? I tried to add it as an item view and I don’t see the app from there after I installed.

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @paulronolo !
Not sure what you mean.

posts.author: CareyLibrary (Carey Hunt)
posts.cookedHtml: This is what we end up doing. Basically, we are having to work twice.

posts.author: mcdonasa (Samantha Lehmond)
posts.cookedHtml: Dealing with this RIGHT now…my team defaults to Slack for proofing because we don’t want our programming units to see all the behind-the-scenes work. (Units are added to the boards so they can see what we’re working on for them - dashboards and boards). As the manager, I do NOT want the proofing process to happen outside monday as we lose the history/development/thought behind our decisions, but need to provide some transparency with our units. Rock - meet Hard Place.

posts.author: mcdonasa (Samantha Lehmond)
posts.cookedHtml: Column permissions won’t help at all. It’s the Update section that needs to be filtered by audience.

posts.author: bade (Mikada)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are happy to introduce the Private Notes and Comments app. That you can keep private notes and internal-only comments under the item view.
You can install the app via link: monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done
You can install the app via Marketplace by Searching Private Notes and Comments

posts.author: SThakkar1 (Subash Thakkar)
posts.cookedHtml: We have achieved this by creating a long text field in Card Details section and giving the permission to that field to board members only

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
I am happy to share that this request is on our upcoming roadmap! We will be adding a view permissions based on roles for update. While there is not an exact timeline available yet, this is expected in the near future. Stay tuned!

Read more about our feature request process here and how your votes help us decide what to work on next.

posts.author: Melissa.martin (Melissa)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Julietteb any updates?

posts.author: Patrmg (Patrmg)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Julietteb
Do you have informations about this feature?
Thank you

posts.author: KatelynCowan (Katelyn Cowan)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Juliette, I’m checking to see how long it will take for this feature to be released. No exact date is necessary, but I’m debating using an integration for this. I’d rather use a native setting and not have to retrain my staff. Any update would be greatly appreciated! Thank you!

posts.author: bade (Mikada)
posts.cookedHtml:
By the meantime you can check our
Private Notes and Comments App New version.
New features;

  • Search comments
  • keeps your private comments and information in monday storage for extra secure and private storage.
  • Reply comments
  • Insert code with desired programming language
  • Mention by clicking the mention button
  • Edit comments
  • Insert link to the comments
  • Change text color
  • Highlight text
  • Insert tables
  • Add emojis to the comments
  • Like comments

posts.author: HM_Capability.Co (Helen)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this one??

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone. I just spoke with the team and they said that the development may take a few more months since they are working on a few different features to upgrade how we interact with our boards. In additional to internal-only comment, this feature will also include ability to hide Updates sections (view permissions) for whole boards.

I will let you Y as soon as there is more information about the timeline for the feature. You can make sure to stay up to date with the status of this feature by turning on notifications for this topic (How to turn on notifications for a topics)
@KatelynCowan @Patrmg @Melissa.martin @HM_Capability.Co

posts.author: georgina8fold (Georgina Daugherty)
posts.cookedHtml: @Chloe_555 its been on the roadmap since 2020 but it will be published in a few more months so fingers crossed!

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml: Any updates on this?

posts.author: mrmarge (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: We’d like to know the updated status as well

posts.author: ChrisH2 (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julietteb Is there any update on this? I can see its been in progress for quite some time and it is a feature that we are desperate for!

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team. As a quick update, we’re currently in Alpha with the Internal-Only Comments feature, which is available only for Enterprise accounts. To clarify, the focus is on enhancing board permissions at the account, workspace, and board levels, so you’ll be able to control who can view updates across these levels. This won’t be applied per update or item yet, but we’re working on making it more granular in the future.
﻿
We really appreciate your continued feedback! We’ll be sure to keep you posted on any progress and next steps.

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
A workaround for this is to create a copy of the item on the client on the internal board. Then you can leave internal-only comments on the items in the internal board.

Let me know if that helps.

posts.author: ChrisH2 (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: We are on an enterprise account. How do we enable this Alpha testing? This feature would be a game changer.

posts.author: mrmarge (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @amybronstein,

Is there any progress about rolling out the feature yet?

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Community! Thank you for your continued patience and feedback. This feature has moved out of development and we are very close to the platform-wide release! We’ll provide an update once this feature is live.

posts.author: mrmarge (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @shannon-cxmonday , do you have an updated timeline for us?

posts.author: Katfooddfw (Kathrine)
posts.cookedHtml: November 20, 2025. Where are we on this?

posts.author: NJE44 (Elizabeth K Coccia)
posts.cookedHtml: Has there been any recent movement on this one? My colleague and I were just talking about this. We were hesitant to invite certain guest users to a board because of the amount of comments/communication available to them

posts.author: popjaco (Poppy)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @shannon-cxmonday any update on this one? I can’t see it yet in platform-wide release. Thanks!
